mission.title: Install and Configure Pipecd on Kubernetes
mission.description: PipeCD is a GitOps style continuous delivery platform that provides a consistent deployment and operations experience for any applications across multiple cloud environments. Installing PipeCD allows you to streamline your deployment processes and enhance your operational efficiency.

steps.title: Clone the PipeCD repository
steps.description:
Clone the repository which contains the installation manifests:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/pipe-cd/pipecd.git
cd pipecd
```

steps.title: Install PipeCD Control Plane
steps.description:
Install PipeCD using the manifests:
```bash
kubectl create namespace pipecd
helm install pipecd manifests/pipecd --namespace pipecd \
  --set quickstart.enabled=true
```

steps.title: Access the PipeCD console
steps.description:
Port-forward to access the PipeCD web console:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/pipecd 8080:8080 -n pipecd
```
Open http://localhost:8080

steps.title: Install Piped (deployment agent)
steps.description:
Download and install the Piped agent on each target environment:
```bash
curl -Lo piped https://github.com/pipe-cd/pipecd/releases/latest/download/piped_linux_amd64
chmod +x piped
sudo mv piped /usr/local/bin/
piped --version
```

resolution.summary: PipeCD control plane is installed with the Piped agent for GitOps-style continuous delivery.

uninstall.title: Remove PipeCD
uninstall.description:
```bash
helm uninstall pipecd -n pipecd
kubectl delete namespace pipecd
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/piped
```

upgrade.title: Pull latest and upgrade
upgrade.description:
```bash
cd pipecd && git pull
helm upgrade pipecd manifests/pipecd --namespace pipecd
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Piped agent
upgrade.description:
```bash
curl -Lo piped https://github.com/pipe-cd/pipecd/releases/latest/download/piped_linux_amd64
chmod +x piped
sudo mv piped /usr/local/bin/
```

troubleshooting.title: Piped agent not connecting
troubleshooting.description: Verify the Piped config points to the correct control plane address and has a valid API key.

troubleshooting.title: Deployment stuck
troubleshooting.description:
Check deployment logs in the PipeCD web console or:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n pipecd
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
